reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: In all previous games, the initial answers provided by the other players for their simple math puzzles have always been perfectly accurate. Passing or verifying carries a 25% risk of random elimination if no one else volunteers, a fate I have suffered multiple times in the past. By volunteering immediately with the initial capsules, I can ensure a correct password is submitted, saving everyone on Round 1 and allowing me to keep my maximum 4 coins.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: As seen in previous games, the puzzles are simple arithmetic problems, making the initial capsules (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D) highly reliable. If I pass or verify, I risk a 25% chance of being randomly eliminated due to no one volunteering. Volunteering immediately using the initial answers is the safest and most optimal move. It completely eliminates the 25% random death risk, ensures I keep all 4 of my coins, and secures an immediate escape for everyone.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1
REASONING: Volunteering in Round 1 without verifying other capsules is too risky, as an incorrect answer from another player will lead to my immediate death. I will verify Q1 to start building consensus and ensure we have the correct password. I am willing to accept the 25% risk of a random elimination if no one volunteers this round in order to gather information.
